segments.paradiso-19-g116: Che here is an interrogative 'what' introducing the question. Poran is a poetic/archaic form of 'potranno' (3rd person plural future of potere) and is followed by the infinitive dir ('to say'), so together poran dir = 'will be able to say'. Li Perse is the subject with the older plural article li + Persians, and a' vostri regi is the dative/complement 'to your kings' (a' is the preposition a with an elision). The word order places the interrogative and verb before the subject for rhetorical effect.
segments.paradiso-19-g117: Come introduces a temporal/conditional clause ('as'/'when') and vedranno is the future (3rd pl.) of vedere: 'when they will see'. Quel volume aperto is the direct object: quel (that) + volume (book/volume) with the participle aperto postnominal functioning as an adjective ('open volume'). Nel qual is the relative 'in which', and si scrivon uses the impersonal/passive si + 3rd-plural verb scrivon ('are written' / 'are inscribed') — so 'in which are written all his/ their dispregi'.
segments.paradiso-19-g118: Lì is an adverb meaning "there" (place). si vedrà is an impersonal/passive-like construction: si + future vedrà (from vedere) yields "one will see" or "will be seen there"; si here is the impersonal/reflective particle and vedrà is the third-person future.
segments.paradiso-19-g119: tra + definite article + opere = "among the works"; l' is the elided definite article il, and opere is the plural noun for literary/creative works (here Alberto's corpus). d' (di) marks possession/attribution: "of Alberto."
segments.paradiso-19-g120: quella is a demonstrative pronoun "that (one)" introducing the specific work; che is the relative pronoun "which/that" linking to the relative clause; tosto is an adverb "soon/quickly;" moverà is the third-person singular future of movare ("will move/put in motion"); la penna literally "the pen" (writing instrument), here personified as an instrument of writing.
segments.paradiso-19-g121: per che here introduces a result/cause relation — "by which/so that/through which." ’l (elided il) regno di Praga = "the kingdom of Prague." fia is an archaic/poetic form used by Dante as a future/optative of essere (here best read as "will be"); diserto is an adjective "deserted".
segments.paradiso-19-g122: Again Lì si vedrà uses the impersonal si + future to mean "there will be seen" or "one will see there." il duol is the noun "the sorrow/grief." che introduces a relative clause; sovra Senna = "over/above the Seine," placing the action geographically; induce is third-person singular present ("causes, induces").
segments.paradiso-19-g123: falseggiando is a gerund/participle (present adverbial) meaning "by falsifying/forging" and modifies the action of the verb in the previous clause; la moneta = "the coin/money" is the object being falsified.
segments.paradiso-19-g124: quel ("that one/the one") begins a further specification; che morrà is a relative clause with morrà the third-person future "will die." di colpo is an idiom meaning "suddenly" (literally "by a blow"), and di cotenna literally "of the hide/skin" — cotenna is "rind/skin;" the phrase indicates a sudden death by a blow to the hide (literal or idiomatic).
segments.paradiso-19-g125: Lì si vedrà again = "there will be seen." la superbia is the noun "pride/arrogance." ch' is the elided relative che "which/that." asseta is third-person singular present (poetic/archaic form) meaning roughly "sets/establishes/takes hold" (from assestare/assettare); here it describes what the pride does or how it stands.
segments.paradiso-19-g126: che introduces a relative/result clause; fa is the third-person singular present of fare "makes/causes." lo Scotto e l’Inghilese are national nouns "the Scot and the Englishman" (the article is retained for each), and folle is the adjective "mad/foolish," predicated of them by fa.
segments.paradiso-19-g127: sì che introduces a result clause "so that/with the result that." non può soffrir = "cannot bear/tolerate" (può = third-person singular present of potere; soffrir here means both "to suffer" and more exactly "to tolerate"). dentro a sua meta = "within his/their proper limit/goal" (dentro = within, meta = goal/limit).
segments.paradiso-19-g73: This segment is just punctuation (a single period). It carries no lexical material to parse or translate; it marks a pause or end of a previous utterance.
segments.paradiso-19-g74: "Or" is an adversative/temporal particle meaning 'now' or 'then' in speech; "tu" is the 2nd-person singular subject 'you'; "chi se’" is a colloquial/poetic contraction of "chi sei" (chi + sei), i.e. 'who are you'. The enclitic apostrophe in "se’" marks the dropped final vowel (sei → se').
segments.paradiso-19-g75: "che" is the relative pronoun introducing the subordinate clause ('who' / 'that'); "vuo’" is a poetic contraction of "vuoi" (2nd-person singular present of volere) meaning 'you want'; "sedere" is the infinitive 'to sit' (in older spelling the final -e is often dropped); "a scranna" is a prepositional phrase meaning 'on/at a bench/high seat' ("a" + "scranna"). The clause reads 'who want to sit on a bench'.
segments.paradiso-19-g76: "per" here expresses purpose/intent 'in order to'; "giudicar" is the (poetic) infinitive form of "giudicare" 'to judge' (final -e dropped); "di lungi mille miglia" is an idiomatic phrase 'from far off, a thousand miles away' ("di" + "lungi" = 'from afar'); "con la veduta corta d'una spanna" is an instrumental phrase 'with the short/narrow sight of a handspan' ("d'" = "di"). The whole string criticizes judging at great distance with a very limited viewpoint.
segments.paradiso-19-g77: "Certo" is an adverb meaning 'certainly' or 'indeed'; "a colui" is a dative phrase 'to that man / to the one'; "che" is the relative pronoun 'who'; "meco" is an archaic prepositional pronoun meaning 'with me' (modern "con me"); "s'" is the reflexive clitic for third person and attaches to the verb; "assottiglia" is present indicative (3rd sing.) of "assottigliare" used reflexively here ('becomes/thins/weakens' or 'diminishes [himself or the force of something]'). In context the clause addresses 'the one who, with me, makes himself smaller/argues with me'.
segments.paradiso-19-g78: "se" would normally be the subordinate conjunction 'if' introducing a conditional, but the provided senses list does not include that meaning (see senses mapping); "la Scrittura" is 'the Scripture' or 'the written text' (here Scripture = Holy Writ) as the subject; "sovra voi" is an older/prepositional phrase 'about you' or 'against you' ("sovra" = 'about/on/over'); "non fosse" is the imperfect subjunctive of "essere" ('were not'), used in a contrary-to-fact conditional 'if Scripture were not against you'.
segments.paradiso-19-g79: "da" here introduces the infinitive phrase (roughly 'to' or 'for') tied to 'dubitar' ('to doubt'); "dubitar" is the (poetic) infinitive of "dubitare" 'to doubt' (final -e omitted); "sarebbe" is the conditional of "essere" ('it would be'); "a maraviglia" is an adverbial phrase 'to wonder' / 'a cause for astonishment' — the whole expression means 'it would indeed be astonishing to doubt (it)'. Note the inverted/impersonal word order: the matter is presented impersonally ('it would be to wonder to doubt').
segments.paradiso-19-g80: "Oh" is a vocative/interjection addressing the hearers; "terreni" is an adjective 'earthly' (plural) qualifying "animali"; "animali" is a plural noun 'animals' or 'creatures' — together a reproachful apostrophe 'O earthly creatures/animals!'.
segments.paradiso-19-g81: 'oh' is an interjection (vocative/exclamatory) addressing the addressees; 'menti' is the plural of 'mente' (minds) serving as the vocative/directly addressed noun; 'grosse' is an attributive adjective agreeing in gender and number with 'menti' and conveys the idea 'thick/slow-witted' here.
segments.paradiso-19-g82: A definite noun phrase 'La prima volontà' (the first will) with the ordinal adjective 'prima' preceding the noun; functions as the sentence's subject and is set off by the following comma indicating a nonrestrictive clause.
segments.paradiso-19-g83: 'ch’ è' is the contraction of 'che è' (which is) introducing a relative clause; 'da sé' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'by itself/in itself' (da + reflexive 'sé'); 'buona' is a predicative adjective agreeing with 'volontà' (feminine) and means 'good' in the moral/ontological sense.
segments.paradiso-19-g84: A repeated prepositional phrase 'da sé' ('of/ by itself') used parenthetically for emphasis; the phrase functions adverbially to reinforce the idea already expressed in the previous clause.
segments.paradiso-19-g85: Another relative clause 'ch’è sommo ben' ('which is the highest good'); 'sommo' is an attributive adjective 'supreme/highest' placed before the noun 'ben' (an abstract noun here meaning 'the good', not the adverb 'well').
segments.paradiso-19-g86: 'Mai non si mosse' is a negative perfective statement: 'mai' with 'non' yields 'never'; 'si' is the reflexive pronoun used with 'mosse'; 'mosse' is the passato remoto (past simple) of 'muovere' ('to move'), so the clause means 'never moved' (i.e., it was never set in motion).
segments.paradiso-19-g87: 'Cotanto' ('to such an extent' / 'so') modifies 'è giusto' ('is just/proper'); 'quanto a lei consuona' is a comparative/relative clause meaning 'as much as befits/suits her' where 'a lei' is a dative of reference and 'consuona' is present indicative of 'consuonare' ('to be in accord / fit').
segments.paradiso-19-g88: 'Nullo creato bene' ('no created good') is the subject (literally 'no created good/thing'); 'a sé la tira' literally 'draws her to itself' — 'a sé' indicates direction/toward itself, 'la' is the feminine direct object pronoun ('her' = the first will), and 'tira' is present indicative 3rd sg. of 'tirare' ('pulls/draws').
segments.paradiso-19-g89: 'Ma' introduces the contrast ('but'); 'essa' is an emphatic feminine pronoun referring back to 'la prima volontà' (the will) and functions as the subject of the following clause.
segments.paradiso-19-g90: 'Radïando' is a present participle (gerundive/gerund form) from 'radïare' ('to radiate'); the dieresis indicates separate pronunciation of the 'i'; it describes the manner in which 'essa' acts: 'radiating' or 'by radiating'.
segments.paradiso-19-g91: 'Lui' is a masculine pronoun here referring back to the 'creato bene' (the created good); 'cagiona' is present indicative 3rd sg. of 'cagionare' ('to cause' / 'to produce'), so the clause means 'she, radiating, causes/produces him' (i.e., the created good originates from her).
segments.paradiso-19-g92: This is the opening of a simile: 'Quale' = 'as/just as' introduces the comparison. 'sovresso il nido' places the adverbial 'over/above the nest' before the noun for poetic emphasis. 'si rigira' is a reflexive present (or timeless habitual) verb meaning 'turns itself/turns back'; the reflexive 'si' marks the action back upon the subject. The phrase 'poi c’ha pasciuti la cicogna i figli' is somewhat inverted for poetic word order: literally 'after (poi) that (c’) has fed (ha pasciuti) the stork the young' — normally 'la cicogna ha pasciuti i figli' — so subject (la cicogna) follows the verb and 'pasciuti' is a past participle used with avere.
segments.paradiso-19-g93: Continuing the simile: 'e come' = 'and as'. 'quel ch’è pasto' is a relative clause where 'ch’' = 'who/which' and 'è' is the copula; here 'pasto' functions as a past/adjectival state ('having eaten' / 'being fed') though the lemma list gives only 'meal' senses. 'la rimira' has 'la' as the direct object (referring back to the stork) and 'rimira' is a present-tense verb 'gazes upon / looks at.'
segments.paradiso-19-g94: 'cotal' = 'such/so' and 'si fece' is a reflexive perfect (literally 'made itself' / 'became so'); 'fece' is passato remoto of 'fare' used here in the sense 'became / was made' (reflexive 'si' gives the middle-passive sense). The clause resumes the main clause after the simile.
segments.paradiso-19-g95: 'e sì leväi i cigli' = 'and thus I raised my eyelids.' 'leväi' (old orthography levai with diaeresis) is first-person past (passato remoto) 'I raised'; subject 'I' is implicit. 'i cigli' (direct object) means 'the eyelids' (poetic for eyes opening).
segments.paradiso-19-g96: Simple noun phrase in apposition or identification: 'la benedetta imagine' = 'the blessed image/vision.' Article + adjective + noun; 'imagine' here denotes a visible apparition or likeness.
segments.paradiso-19-g97: Relative clause introduced by 'che' ('which') modifying 'imagine.' 'l’ali movea' has the article before 'ali' (wings) and 'movea' is the imperfect 'was moving' (descriptive past). 'sospinte da tanti consigli' is a participial phrase: 'sospinte' (past participle 'pushed/driven') agrees with 'ali' and 'da' marks the agent ('by so many counsels').
segments.paradiso-19-g98: 'Roteando' is an adverbial gerund ('revolving'/'turning') describing a simultaneous action; it precedes the main verb as a poetic adverbial phrase. 'cantava' is the imperfect indicative (3rd person singular) meaning 'was singing' or 'kept singing', an ongoing/past action whose subject is left implicit.
segments.paradiso-19-g99: 'e' simply coordinates with the previous clause ('and'). 'dicea' is the imperfect indicative (3rd person singular) 'was saying' or 'said' in a narrated past; it introduces the following direct speech.
segments.paradiso-19-g100: Word order is inverted for a simile: 'Quali son le mie note a te' = 'As my notes are to you'. 'Quali' functions as 'as/such as' here; 'son' (archaic present of 'sono') is 3rd-person plural 'are'; the definite article 'le' appears before the possessive 'mie' as is normal in Italian; 'a te' is a dative/oblique 'to you' or 'for you'.
segments.paradiso-19-g101: 'che' introduces a relative clause 'who/that'; 'non' negates the verb; 'le' here is the feminine plural object pronoun 'them', referring back to 'note'; '’ntendi' is the contracted/poetic form of 'intendi' (2nd-person singular present) 'you understand'. Together: 'who do not understand them.'
segments.paradiso-19-g102: 'tal è' = 'such is' / 'so is'; 'il giudicio etterno' is the noun phrase 'the eternal judgment' (etterno = archaic/poetic spelling of 'eterno'); 'a voi mortali' gives the dative/beneficiary 'to you mortals' (plural). The whole clause states the simile's result: 'so is the eternal judgment to you mortals.'
segments.paradiso-19-g103: 'Poi' = 'then/afterwards'. 'si quetaro' is a reflexive past (archaic form of 'si quetarono') meaning 'quieted themselves' or 'settled down' (3rd person plural passato remoto). 'quei lucenti incendi' = 'those shining fires' (incendi = 'fires/blazes' used literally or metaphorically). 'de lo' = archaic 'del' ('of the'); 'Spirito Santo' = 'Holy Spirit'. 'ancor' = 'still/again'; 'nel segno' = 'in the sign/standard'; the relative 'che fé i Romani al mondo reverendi' = 'which made the Romans reverenced/venerable in the world' (fé = passato remoto of fare 'made').
segments.paradiso-19-g104: 'esso' is a neuter/impersonal pronoun 'it' (referring back to the entity that had been speaking); 'ricominciò' is passato remoto 3rd singular 'resumed' or 'began again', introducing the next speech act.
segments.paradiso-19-g105: 'A questo regno' = 'to/into this kingdom' (a + noun). 'non salì mai' uses passato remoto 'salì' (3rd singular) with negation and 'mai' to mean 'never ascended' or 'never entered/attained'. The relative 'chi non credette ’n Cristo' ('he who did not believe in Christ') identifies who never ascended; 'credette' is passato remoto of 'credere'.
segments.paradiso-19-g106: Paired negative 'né... né' = 'neither... nor'. 'pria' = 'before'; 'poi' = 'after'. 'ch’el' = 'that he/when he'; 'si' is reflexive marking a passive or middle construction; 'chiavasse' is an archaic subjunctive/imperfect-subjunctive form (here expressing the temporal clause 'before nor after he was nailed'), from 'chiavare' used to mean 'to nail'/'to fasten' (contextually 'nailed to the wood' = crucified); 'al legno' = 'to the wood' (the cross).
segments.paradiso-19-g107: 'Ma vedi:' is an interjectional address: 'Ma' = 'but' introduces a turn, and 'vedi' (second person singular present) functions as 'see/you see' — here used vocatively to call the listener's attention.
segments.paradiso-19-g108: 'molti gridan' = 'many shout' — 'molti' is the plural noun/adjective 'many' and 'gridan' is the third-person plural present 'they shout/cry out.' The following quoted word begins the shouted exclamation.
segments.paradiso-19-g109: Repetition of the exclamation 'Cristo!' The quoted vocative/name is the object of the shouting; here it functions as an invocation/cry rather than as syntactic subject or predicate.
segments.paradiso-19-g110: This segment is only the punctuation comma, marking the continuation of the preceding clause into a subordinate relative clause; it does not contain words to analyse.
segments.paradiso-19-g111: "che" is a relative pronoun referring back to the people just mentioned. "saranno" is the future indicative (third person plural) of essere and marks a future-tense assessment. The prepositional phrase "in giudicio" (= "in judgment") gives the sphere in which they will be positioned; "assai men prope" is a comparative phrase where "assai" intensifies "men" (less) and "prope" means "near/close to"; finally "a lui" is the prepositional complement meaning "to him."
segments.paradiso-19-g112: The first "che" here functions as the comparative "than." "tal" (= "such") introduces the example being compared; the second "che" is a relative pronoun meaning "who/which" and begins the subordinate clause. "non conosce" is present indicative (third person singular) of conoscere, giving a general characteristic, and "Cristo" is its direct object (here the historical Jesus, not an expletive).
segments.paradiso-19-g113: "e" = 'and' links this clause to the previous comparative. "tai" is an archaic/poetic form meaning 'such' (pointing back to the type just described). "Cristian" is a noun 'Christian/person' used here as a generic representative; "dannerà" is the future indicative, third person singular, of dannare and means 'will damn/condemn.' The contracted article "l'" before "Etïòpe" introduces the direct object 'the Ethiopian.' Note Dante uses a singular form to express a generalizing action (each such Christian will condemn).
segments.paradiso-19-g114: "quando" is the temporal conjunction 'when.' "si partiranno" is the future tense (third person plural) of partire with the reflexive pronoun "si," meaning 'they will separate' or 'they will part from one another.' "i due collegi" is the plural subject 'the two colleges/groups' that will split; word order places the reflexive verb before the subject for rhythm and emphasis.
segments.paradiso-19-g115: This is a balanced correlative: "l'uno ... e l'altro" = 'one ... and the other.' "in etterno" uses the poetic/archaic adjective "etterno" ('eternal') with the preposition "in" to indicate the state in eternity; "ricco" is 'rich.' The contrasted "inòpe" (from Latin inops) is an archaic adjective meaning 'needy/poor.' The phrase as a whole contrasts the eternal fate of the two groups.
segments.paradiso-19-g58: A short, inverted copular clause: the noun 'Lume' (predicate noun) comes before the verb 'è' with the negator 'non' before the verb, so literally 'Light is not' => 'It is not light.' The subject is implicit; Dante places the noun first for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-19-g59: 'se non' here functions as 'unless/except if.' 'vien' is the archaic poetic form of 'viene' (3rd person singular present of venire: 'comes'); 'dal' = da + il 'from the'; 'sereno' is used as a noun meaning the clear/serene sky. The relative clause 'che non si turba mai' uses the reflexive 'si turba' (present indicative, passive/reflexive sense) with 'non...mai' = 'never'.
segments.paradiso-19-g60: 'anzi' introduces a corrective contrast ('rather' / 'on the contrary'). 'è' is the copula. Dante links two alternatives with the archaic 'od' (variant of 'o') — 'tenèbra' (darkness) or 'ombra de la carne' (a shadow of the flesh) — and finishes with 'suo veleno' where the possessive 'suo' refers back to 'carne' ('its poison'). Note the use of 'de' (archaic 'di').
segments.paradiso-19-g61: 'Assai' (much/enough) modifies the following predicate; the structure 't'è mo aperta la latebra' literally 'to you is now opened the hiding-place' — Dante uses the clitic 't’' (to you) before the verb and the past participle/adjective 'aperta' after the copula 'è.' 'mo' = now. The relative 'che t’ascondeva la giustizia viva' uses the imperfect 'ascondeva' (was hiding/concealed) with the clitic object 't’' (= 'from you'), and 'giustizia viva' = 'living/divine justice.'
segments.paradiso-19-g62: 'di che' = 'about which / concerning which' (referring back to the latebra). 'facei' is an archaic imperfect form meaning 'you used to make' or 'you made repeatedly' (the subject 'tu' is implicit). 'question' is a feminine noun 'question/dispute' and 'cotanto crebra' modifies it: 'cotanto' = 'so much/so often' and 'crebra' = 'frequent' (Dante's form agreeing with the feminine 'question').
segments.paradiso-19-g63: 'ché' here = 'for' / 'because' introducing the reason clause. 'tu' is the explicit subject, and 'dicevi' is the imperfect 'you used to say' (habitual past).
segments.paradiso-19-g64: Reported speech begins. 'Un uom nasce' uses the present 'nasce' (is born / arises) to state a general fact. 'a la riva' = 'on the shore' (preposition 'a' + article), and 'de l’Indo' = 'of the Indus' (de = di + article). Note the elided 'l’' before 'Indo'.
segments.paradiso-19-g65: 'e quivi non è chi' = 'and there is no one who...' The construction 'non è chi + subjunctive' (here 'ragioni', 'legga', 'scriva') is a Dantean/archaic pattern meaning 'there is no one who would/does ...'; the verbs are present subjunctive forms indicating potential/characteristic action. 'quivi' = there, and the list 'né chi legga né chi scriva' repeats the 'chi' + subjunctive pattern for 'read' and 'write.'
segments.paradiso-19-g66: Inverted order with the copula at the end: 'e tutti suoi voleri e atti buoni sono' = 'and all his wills/wishes and good acts are...' 'tutti' quantifies the plural subject, 'suoi' is the possessive 'his,' and 'buoni' qualifies the acts.
segments.paradiso-19-g67: 'quanto' = 'as much as' / 'to the extent that'; here it introduces a limiting clause 'as far as human reason sees.' 'ragione umana' is the subject phrase and 'vede' (3rd person singular present of vedere) means 'sees' or 'can perceive/judge.' Word order (quanto + noun + adjective + verb) is typical poetic inversion.
segments.paradiso-19-g68: 'sanza' is the archaic 'senza' = 'without.' 'peccato' = 'sin'; 'in vita o in sermoni' qualifies the absence of sin 'either in his life or in his words/speeches.' The parallel prepositional phrases ('in vita' / 'in sermoni') close the subordinate clause.
segments.paradiso-19-g69: 'Muore' is 3rd person singular present 'dies' used generically; the participial phrase 'non battezzato' ('not baptized') follows the verb to describe the state at death. 'e sanza fede' adds 'and without faith' — 'sanza' = archaic 'without', and 'fede' = 'faith.'
segments.paradiso-19-g70: Rhetorical question beginning: 'ov’ è questa giustizia che ’l condanna?' = 'Where is this justice that condemns him?' 'ov’' = 'ovo' (where), 'questa giustizia' = 'this justice,' and 'che ’l condanna' is a relative clause with the clitic '’l' = 'lo' (him) object of the verb 'condanna' (condemns).
segments.paradiso-19-g71: Another rhetorical question: 'ov’ è la colpa sua,' = 'where is his fault/guilt?' 'colpa' = fault/guilt; 'sua' is the possessive 'his.' Word order places the predicate after 'ov’' for emphasis.
segments.paradiso-19-g72: 'se ei non crede?' = 'if he does not believe?' 'se' = 'if' (introducing the condition). 'ei' is an archaic subject pronoun = 'he'; 'crede' is 3rd person singular present of 'credere' (believe). The clause is a direct continuation of the rhetorical question.
segments.paradiso-19-g1: Parea is an imperfect indicative (archaic spelling of pareva) meaning 'seemed/appeared' and opens the clause; the subject la bella image follows the verb (inverted word order common in Italian poetry). Dinanzi a me is a prepositional phrase 'before/in front of me'; con l’ali aperte is an accompanying circumstance ('with the wings open') modifying how the image appeared. The relative clause che ... facevan modifies image: frui here is a noun ('the enjoyment'/'fruition'), facevan is the archaic imperfect of fare ('they made/they rendered'), and conserte is a past participle/adjective meaning 'joined/harmonized'.
segments.paradiso-19-g2: Again parea ('seemed') with an inverted subject ciascuna ('each one'); rubinetto is used metaphorically ('little spout'/'nozzle') and is the predicate complement. The relative in cui ('in which') introduces a subordinate clause; raggio di sole is the phrase 'a ray of the sun' and ardesse is an imperfect-subjunctive form of ardere ('would burn/was burning'), used in a vivid, image-making subordinate clause; sì acceso is an adverb + participle phrase 'so inflamed/so blazing'.
segments.paradiso-19-g3: Che introduces a further relative clause linked to the preceding image; ne here is a locative/partitive pronoun ('in/of it' → 'there/in them') combined with miei occhi ('my eyes') to mean 'in my eyes'. Rifrangesse is an imperfect-subjunctive of rifrangere ('to refract'/'to be broken by refraction'), used to express the resulting visual effect, and lui is the emphatic pronoun 'him/it' referring back to the thing being refracted.
segments.paradiso-19-g4: Quel che = 'that which'; mi convien is an impersonal modal construction ('it is necessary for me') followed by the infinitive ritrar (archaic form of ritrarre/ritrarre 'to depict/describe'). Testeso is an archaic/adverbial word meaning roughly 'right here/at once' or 'in full' modifying the infinitive. The whole phrase means 'and that which I must describe here at once'.
segments.paradiso-19-g5: Non portò voce mai is literal: portò (past of portare) with voce ('voice / word') means 'never bore/was never given voice'; non ... mai = 'never'. This is a simple negative past statement emphasizing that it had no audible public report.
segments.paradiso-19-g6: Né scrisse incostro: né = 'nor'; scrisse is the perfect past of scrivere ('was written'/'wrote'); incostro is an uncommon/archaic adverb meaning roughly 'in our presence' or 'before us' (here the supplied sense lists do not match perfectly). The phrase means 'nor was it written before/in our presence.'
segments.paradiso-19-g7: Né fu per fantasia già mai compreso: fu is the passato remoto of essere ('was'); per fantasia = 'by imagination' (i.e. not imagined); già mai here functions as 'ever' in a negative context meaning 'ever'/'at any time'; compreso is past participle of comprendere used intransitively as 'grasped/understood'. The line says it was never grasped by imagination.
segments.paradiso-19-g8: Ch’io vidi e anche udi’ parlar lo rostro: ch’ = che ('that'); vidi is passato remoto of vedere ('I saw'); anche adds 'even'; udi’ is an archaic/apocope form of udii/udì ('I heard') and is followed by the infinitive parlar ('speak'), while lo rostro ('the face/visage') is the grammatical subject of parlar. Together: 'for I saw and even heard the visage speak.'
segments.paradiso-19-g9: E sonar ne la voce e «io» e «mio»: sonar here means 'to sound' or 'to be heard'; ne la voce = 'in the voice' (ne = locative/partitive pronoun); the quoted words «io» and «mio» are said to 'sound' in that voice. The structure reports what was heard in the voice.
segments.paradiso-19-g10: Quand’ era nel concetto e ‘noi’ e ‘nostro’: quand’ (quando) + era (imperfect of essere) 'when it was' nel concetto = 'in the conception/thought'; the clause contrasts spoken singular possessives ('I' and 'my') with the notion that in thought it was actually 'we' and 'our'.
segments.paradiso-19-g11: E cominciò is simple narrative: E = 'and'; cominciò is passato remoto of cominciare ('he began').
segments.paradiso-19-g12: Per esser giusto e pio son io qui essaltato a quella gloria: Per esser = 'for being'; esser is the infinitive, giusto e pio are predicate adjectives describing the speaker. Son io (inverted order) is 'I am' (son = son/sono) and essaltato is a past participle used adjectivally/passively 'exalted.' A quella gloria is the target ('to that glory'). The relative clause che non si lascia vincere a disio uses the impersonal/reflexive si + lasciare + infinitive vincere ('does not let itself be overcome') and a + disio ('by/desire') to mean the glory is not overcome by desire.
segments.paradiso-19-g13: E in terra lasciai la mia memoria sì fatta: e = 'and'; in terra = 'on earth'; lasciai is passato remoto of lasciare ('I left/left behind'); la mia memoria = 'my memory/reputation'; sì fatta is a participial phrase 'so made/so fashioned'. Grammatically, lasciai takes a direct object la mia memoria modified by the participle phrase.
segments.paradiso-19-g14: Che le genti lì malvage commendan lei: che introduces a result/explanatory clause ('so that'); le genti lì malvage = 'the people there, wicked'; commendan is present tense (archaic spelling of commendano) meaning 'they commend/praise' and lei is the direct object 'it/her' (referring to memoria).
segments.paradiso-19-g15: Ma non seguon la storia: ma = 'but'; non seguon (seguono) = 'they do not follow' (present tense, archaic spelling); la storia = 'the story/history'—a simple contrasting clause: people praise the reputation but do not follow its course/true story.
segments.paradiso-19-g16: Così introduces a comparison ('thus/so'); un sol calor = 'one single heat/warmth' (sol = 'single/only'); di molte brage = 'from many brage' (brage is a plural noun; in context it's a material source of warmth). Si fa sentir is a reflexive/passive construction meaning 'is made to be felt' or more simply 'is felt' (si + fa + infinitive indicates becoming/causal passivity).
segments.paradiso-19-g17: Come di molti amori usciva solo un suon di quella image: come = 'as' introduces the simile; di molti amori = 'from many loves/affections'; usciva is imperfect of uscire ('issued/came forth'), and solo un suon = 'only one sound'; di quella image = 'from that image' — the line parallels the single warmth made from many sources with a single sound issuing from many affections in the image.
segments.paradiso-19-g18: 'Ond’' is a contracted, poetic form of 'onde' meaning 'thereupon' or 'therefore'; it introduces what the speaker now says. The sequence 'io appresso' places the emphatic pronoun 'io' before the adverb 'appresso' ('next/afterwards'), a common Dantean word order that signals 'then I (next) ...'.
segments.paradiso-19-g19: This is a vocative address: 'O perpetüi fiori' = 'O perpetual flowers'. 'de l’' is the archaic form of 'di + il' (of the), and 'l’etterna letizia' is a genitive phrase 'of the eternal joy'. The adjective 'perpetüi' (archaic spelling) modifies the plural noun 'fiori'.
segments.paradiso-19-g20: 'che' introduces a relative clause describing the 'fiori'. 'pur' is an adverb of concession/emphasis ('still, even'). The unusual word order 'uno parer mi fate' is literal: 'you make (fate) seem (parer) one (uno) to me (mi)' — i.e. 'all your scents appear as one to me.' 'Fate' is present indicative, 2nd-person plural, governing the infinitive/periphrasis with 'parer'.
segments.paradiso-19-g21: 'Solvetemi' is an older/poetic form with the enclitic object 'mi' ('me'). Here it functions as an appeal meaning 'release/loosen me' or 'dissolve for me' and is addressed to the addressees (the flowers), so read as an imperative/request. The verb takes the following noun phrase ('il gran digiuno') as its object.
segments.paradiso-19-g22: 'Spirando' is a present participle (gerund) from 'spirare' and describes the manner or means: 'by breathing' or 'as you breathe/exhale'. It links the action of the addressees' breathing to the requested release of the fast.
segments.paradiso-19-g23: 'il gran digiuno' is the direct object ('the great fast'). The relative clause that follows, introduced by 'che', contains the compound tense 'm’ha tenuto' (auxiliary 'ha' + past participle 'tenuto'), meaning 'has kept me'; 'lungamente' modifies the duration. Here 'm’' is the clitic object referring to the speaker ('me').
segments.paradiso-19-g24: This is an attendant-circumstance gerund clause with a negative: 'non trovandoli' ('not finding them/it'), where the enclitic '-li' attaches to the gerund. It expresses the reason/circumstance: 'not finding any food on earth.' 'Alcuno' is negated by the leading 'non' (hence understood as 'no / any at all').
segments.paradiso-19-g25: 'Ben' is an adverbial intensifier ('well/indeed') and precedes the verb for emphasis: 'Ben so io che' = 'I well know that'. The order 'so io' (verb + pronoun) is emphatic/poetic; 'che' introduces the subordinate clause.
segments.paradiso-19-g26: 'Se' is the conditional 'if' and here begins a protasis. '’n' is a contraction of 'in' (written with an apostrophe), so '’n cielo' = 'in heaven'. 'Altro reame' ('another realm') is the subject; 'la divina giustizia fa suo specchio' is a present indicative clause ('makes divine justice its mirror'). Word order is fairly standard subject–verb–object, with the article 'la' before 'divina giustizia'.
segments.paradiso-19-g27: The second 'che' introduces the result/consequence clause. '’l vostro' = 'your (realm)' with the article 'l’' before 'vostro' (archaic order 'l' + possessive). 'Non l’apprende' = 'does not take/receive/learn it' — 'l’' before 'apprende' is the unstressed direct-object clitic referring back to what was just mentioned (justice/specchio). 'Con velame' ('with a veil') is a prepositional phrase adverbially qualifying how it would be received; the verb 'apprende' is present indicative, 3rd sing.
segments.paradiso-19-g28: 'Sapete' is 2nd-person plural present ('you know'). 'Come' here is an interrogative/relative adverb meaning 'how'. 'Attento' (adjective) before the subject emphasizes manner: 'how attentively'. 'Io m’apparecchio' is the reflexive verb 'apparecchiarsi' ('I ready/prepare myself'), with the enclitic 'm’' (me/myself). 'Ad ascoltar' uses 'ad' + infinitive (a common poetic alternative to 'a + infinitive') meaning 'to listen'.
segments.paradiso-19-g29: The second clause repeats 'sapete' for emphasis: 'you know what that doubt is'. 'Qual è quello dubbio' is a nominal phrase 'what/which is that doubt'; the relative clause 'che m’è digiun cotanto vecchio' uses 'm’' as the indirect-clitic 'to me' in the expression 'm'è digiun' — literally 'is (a) fast to me' — i.e. 'has been a long-standing deprivation/famine for me.' 'Cotanto vecchio' = 'so/so very old' (i.e. so long-standing).
segments.paradiso-19-g30: 'Quasi' introduces a simile ('as if' / 'like'). 'Falcone' is the simile's noun ('falcon'), and 'ch’ esce del cappello' is a relative clause ('that comes out of the cap/hat'); 'ch’' is the contracted 'che'. 'Esce' is present indicative, 3rd person singular, used vividly for the falcon's motion.
segments.paradiso-19-g31: 'Move la testa' is simple present-action: 'he moves/turns the head'. The coordinate 'e con l’ali si plaude' uses the reflexive clitic 'si' with 'plaude' (present indicative) to describe the wings' beating — 'with the wings he claps/beats himself', i.e. 'beats his wings'. The prepositional 'con l’ali' marks the instrument ('with the wings').
segments.paradiso-19-g32: Two gerund/participle phrases describe manner: 'voglia mostrando' ('showing desire') and 'faccendosi bello' ('making himself beautiful' / 'adorning himself'). 'Voglia' is a noun 'desire/will'; 'mostrando' and 'faccendosi' are present-participial forms indicating simultaneous actions. 'Faccendosi' is reflexive ('making oneself').
segments.paradiso-19-g33: 'Vid’' is the contracted poetic form of 'vidi' (shortened past 'I saw'). The construction 'vid' io farsi quel segno' uses the infinitive 'farsi' in an accusative-infinitive structure: 'I saw that sign being made' (the infinitive expresses the action witnessed). 'Quel segno' is the direct object.
segments.paradiso-19-g34: The relative 'che' introduces a clause explaining the nature of the sign: 'che ... era contesto' = 'which was filled/embroidered/imbued with praise of divine grace.' 'Di laude de la divina grazia' is a genitive/partitive expression 'of praises of divine grace'. 'Era' is imperfect of 'essere' (was). 'Contesto' is a poetic past-participle/adjectival use meaning 'surrounded/filled/imbued' (archaic usage).
segments.paradiso-19-g35: 'Con canti' = 'with songs'. 'Quai' is an archaic/dialectal form (contextual: 'such as' / 'which'), followed by 'si sa chi là sù gaude' — a relative clause meaning 'as those who rejoice up there know.' 'Si sa' is an impersonal/idiomatic 'one knows' or 'is known', and 'gaude' (present) describes the people 'up there' who rejoice.
segments.paradiso-19-g36: Poi cominciò is a simple narrative past: 'Poi' = 'then/afterwards' followed by cominciò, passato remoto of cominciare meaning 'he began'. The colon that follows introduces direct speech.
segments.paradiso-19-g37: Colui che volse il sesto a lo stremo del mondo is a relative clause. 'Colui' is the demonstrative-substantive 'he who'; 'che' introduces the relative clause; 'volse' is passato remoto of volgere meaning 'turned/directed'; 'il sesto' is 'the sixth' (sphere/level); 'a lo stremo del mondo' = 'to the extremity/limit of the world' (note the poetic separation of the article 'a lo' instead of modern 'allo').
segments.paradiso-19-g38: 'e dentro ad esso distinse tanto occulto e manifesto' — 'e' = 'and'; 'dentro ad esso' = 'within it' (esso refers back to il sesto); 'distinse' is passato remoto of discernere = 'he distinguished/discerned'; 'tanto' qualifies the extent ('so much'); 'occulto e manifesto' are coordinated adjectives 'hidden and manifest'.
segments.paradiso-19-g39: 'non poté suo valor sì fare impresso in tutto l’universo' — 'non poté' (past tense) = 'was not able'; here poté governs the infinitive phrase introduced by 'fare' (make); 'suo valor' = 'his strength/power'; 'sì' = 'so' (to that degree); 'fare impresso' = 'to make impressed/to stamp'; 'in tutto l’universo' = 'throughout the whole universe'.
segments.paradiso-19-g40: 'che ’l suo verbo non rimanesse in infinito eccesso' — 'che' introduces the subordinate/result clause; '’l suo verbo' = 'his Word' (here loaded theological sense: the Word/Logos); 'non rimanesse' is imperfect subjunctive (rimanere) used in a clause depending on the previous negative ability — here translating as 'would not remain'; 'in infinito eccesso' = 'in infinite excess' (adverbial phrase).
segments.paradiso-19-g41: 'E ciò fa certo che ’l primo superbo,' — 'E' = 'and'; 'ciò fa certo' = 'this makes/certifies' or 'this proves/establishes'; 'che' introduces the clause describing what is proved; 'l' primo superbo' is a substantive use of the adjective 'superbo' = 'the first proud (being)'.
segments.paradiso-19-g42: 'che fu la somma d’ogne creatura,' — a relative clause: 'che fu' = 'who was' (passato remoto of essere); 'la somma' = 'the highest/supreme' (somma used as substantive/adjective = summit); 'd’ogne creatura' = 'of every creature' (ogne = archaic form of 'ogni').
segments.paradiso-19-g43: 'per non aspettar lume,' — 'per' here expresses cause ('because of' / 'for'); 'non aspettar' is the infinitive 'aspettare' with archaic final -r, 'not waiting/ not awaiting'; 'lume' = 'light' figuratively 'illumination' or 'divine light'.
segments.paradiso-19-g44: 'cadde acerbo;' — 'cadde' passato remoto of cadere = 'he fell'; 'acerbo' used figuratively = 'bitter/harsh/grievous' (not 'unripe' here), describing the manner or quality of the fall.
segments.paradiso-19-g45: This long clause: 'e quinci appar ch’ogne minor natura è corto recettacolo a quel bene che non ha fine e sé con sé misura.' — 'e quinci appar' = 'and hence it appears'; 'ch’' introduces the content; 'ogne minor natura' = 'every lesser nature' (comparative 'minor' used substantively with 'natura'); 'è corto recettacolo' = 'is a limited/short receptacle' (corto = limited here, recettacolo = receptacle); 'a quel bene' = 'for that Good'; 'che non ha fine' = 'which has no end'; 'e sé con sé misura' = literally 'and measures itself with itself' — reflexive construction meaning the Good's measure is internal to itself. Note the reflexive 'sé con sé' emphasizes self-measure.
segments.paradiso-19-g46: 'Dunque vostra veduta,' — 'Dunque' = 'therefore/so'; 'vostra veduta' = 'your sight/vision' (vostra = possessive; veduta = noun 'sight' or 'way of seeing'). This is the topic phrase for the following relative clause.
segments.paradiso-19-g47: 'che convene esser alcun de’ raggi de la mente di che tutte le cose son ripiene,' — 'che' introduces a clause; 'convene' (archaic spelling of 'conviene') is impersonal/present and means 'it befits/is fitting'; it takes an infinitive 'esser' ('to be'); 'alcun de’ raggi' = 'some (a ray) of the rays' (alcun here = 'some/a certain'); 'de la mente' = 'of the Mind' (the divine Mind); 'di che tutte le cose son ripiene' = 'of which all things are filled' (son ripiene = 'are filled').
segments.paradiso-19-g48: 'non pò da sua natura esser possente tanto,' — negative modal construction: 'non può' = 'cannot'; here spelled 'pò' in older orthography; 'da sua natura' = 'by its nature' (expressing inherent limitation); 'esser possente' = 'be powerful'; 'tanto' = 'so much' / 'to that degree'.
segments.paradiso-19-g49: 'che suo principio discerna molto di là da quel che l’è parvente.' — 'che' introduces the result clause; 'suo principio' = 'its principle/origin'; 'discerna' = present indicative 'discerns/perceives'; 'molto di là da' = 'far beyond'; 'quel che l’è parvente' = 'what appears to it' (parvente = 'seeming/appearing'; l’ = dative/indirect object 'to it' or reflexive 'to itself' depending on referent).
segments.paradiso-19-g50: 'Però ne la giustizia sempiterna la vista che riceve il vostro mondo,' — 'Però' = 'therefore/however'; 'ne la giustizia sempiterna' = 'in the everlasting/eternal justice' (ne = in); 'la vista' = 'the sight/vision'; 'che riceve il vostro mondo' = 'that receives/perceives your world' (riceve = present 'receives' or 'takes in').
segments.paradiso-19-g51: 'com’ occhio per lo mare,' — simile/analogy introduced by 'com’' (short for 'come') = 'as'; 'occhio' = 'an eye'; 'per lo mare' = 'over/regarding the sea' — the vision is compared to an eye looking at the sea.
segments.paradiso-19-g52: 'entro s’interna;' — 'entro' = 'within/inside'; 's’interna' is a pronominal verb form (s' + interna(rsi)) meaning 'it turns inward/withdraws into itself' or 'it internalizes' (the reflexive clitic s’ marks that the action falls on the subject itself).
segments.paradiso-19-g53: Single word 'che,' a conjunction meaning 'for/that' — here introducing the explanatory concession clause that follows.
segments.paradiso-19-g54: 'ben che da la proda veggia il fondo,' — 'ben che' functions as a concessive 'although'; 'da la proda' = 'from the shore/edge'; 'veggia' (archaic) = 'beholds/sees' (used here in the sense of perceiving visually from the shore); 'il fondo' = 'the bottom/depth'.
segments.paradiso-19-g55: 'in pelago nol vede;' — 'in pelago' = 'in the open sea/abyss'; 'nol' = contracting 'non + lo' = 'does not see it'; 'vede' = present 'sees' — together: 'in the open sea it does not see it.'
segments.paradiso-19-g56: 'e nondimeno èli,' — 'e' = 'and'; 'nondimeno' = 'nevertheless'; 'èli' here is an archaic emphatic form meaning 'it/he is (there)' — a short emphatic copula/pronoun combination pointing back to the thing (the bottom/depth) that remains present.
segments.paradiso-19-g57: 'ma cela lui l’esser profondo.' — 'ma' = 'but'; 'cela' = verb celare 'conceals'; 'lui' = dative/indirect pronoun 'from him/it' (referring to the eye/vision); 'l’esser profondo' = 'the deep being/existence' (the substantive infinitive 'esser' + adjective 'profondo'). Syntax: the depth (esser profondo) conceals itself from the perceiver (lui).
segments.paradiso-19-g128: Vedrassi is an impersonal/future form meaning 'one will see' or 'there will be seen' (Dante often uses this impersonal construction). The phrase orders the direct objects before the originating phrase: la lussuria e 'l viver molle = 'the lust and the soft living', where '’l' is the contracted article. 'di quel di Spagna e di quel di Boemme' is a genitive-like phrase meaning 'of those of Spain and of those of Bohemia' (quel di X = 'the one(s) of X' / 'those from X').
segments.paradiso-19-g129: 'che' introduces a relative clause referring back to 'quel di Spagna e ... Boemme' ('who'/'which'). 'mai' with the negative 'non' yields 'never' ('mai non conobbe' = 'never knew'). 'conobbe' is the remote past (passato remoto) of conoscere = 'knew/was acquainted with.' 'né volle' uses 'né' (= 'nor') plus 'volle' (passato remoto of volere) = 'nor did he/she/they wish/choose', so the clause says they neither knew nor desired 'valor'.
segments.paradiso-19-g130: Again the impersonal future 'Vedrassi' = 'one will see'. 'al' (= a + il) is locative here: 'at the Ciotto of Jerusalem.' 'Ciotto' is a proper name/place in this context (hence treated as a name rather than its literal lexical senses). 'segnata' is a past participle agreeing with 'la sua bontate' that follows (inverted order): 'his goodness marked with an i' — 'con un i' expresses the mark made by the letter i.
segments.paradiso-19-g131: 'quando' = 'when' introduces a temporal clause. '’l contrario' = 'the opposite' or 'the contrary one' (i.e., the opposing person/thing). 'segnerà' is the future tense of segnare = 'will mark', and 'un emme' means 'an M' (the letter emme). The clause says: when the contrary will mark it with an M.
segments.paradiso-19-g132: Vedrassi again = 'one will see.' 'l’avarizia e la viltate' = 'the avarice and the cowardice' (two abstract nouns, each with the article). 'di quei che guarda' = 'of those who watch/guard' — 'quei' (those) + relative 'che' (who). 'guarda' is present indicative here in a generic/habitual sense: 'those who guard/watch the island of fire.'
segments.paradiso-19-g133: 'ove' = 'where' (archaic/poetic for 'dove'). 'Anchise' is the proper name Anchises. 'finì' is passato remoto of finire = 'ended' or 'died/finished' and governs 'la lunga etate' = 'his long age/life' (etate = years/age). The clause situates the island as the place where Anchises ended his long years.
segments.paradiso-19-g134: The connective 'e' = 'and' links what follows: 'a dare ad intender quanto è poco' is an infinitival purpose/attitude phrase: 'to give (a sense) / to make one understand how little it is.' Here 'dare' = 'to give', 'ad intender' = 'to be understood' (infinitive), and 'quanto è poco' = 'how little it is.'
segments.paradiso-19-g135: 'la sua scrittura fian lettere mozze' places subject and predicate in unusual order for emphasis: 'la sua scrittura' = 'his writing/text' and 'fian' is the present subjunctive/pluperfect-like form of 'fieri'/'essere' used impersonally here meaning 'will be made' or 'shall be' (Dante uses fian = 'they be' / 'shall be'). 'lettere mozze' = 'truncated (cut-off) letters' (i.e., letters written incompletely).
segments.paradiso-19-g136: 'che noteranno molto in parvo loco' is a relative clause modifying 'lettere mozze': 'which will note/indicate much in a small place.' 'noteranno' is future indicative 'they will note' or 'they will show/record', and 'in parvo loco' = 'in a small/limited space', so the truncated letters will pack a lot of meaning into a little space.
segments.paradiso-19-g137: 'E' = 'and'. 'Parranno' is the future of parere (here 'will appear'/'will seem' or 'will be seen'); Dante uses this older/variant form. 'a ciascun' = 'to each one' (i.e., to everyone). 'l’opere sozze' = 'the filthy/dirty works/deeds' (sozze agreeing with opere). 'del barba e del fratel' = 'of the barber/bearded one and of the brother/friar' — two figures of blame; the preposition 'del' (= di + il) introduces their possession or association.
segments.paradiso-19-g138: 'che' introduces a relative clause describing those men (the barber and the brother): 'tanto egregia nazione e due corone han fatte bozze.' 'tanto' = 'so/so great'; 'egregia' is an adjective meaning 'illustrious/remarkable' (here modifying 'nazione'); 'nazione' = 'nation'; 'e due corone' = 'and two crowns'; 'han fatte' is present perfect 'have made' and 'bozze' = 'rough drafts/sketches' (i.e., they have reduced a noble nation and two crowns to mere drafts/sketches).
segments.paradiso-19-g139: E is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and'. 'quel' is the demonstrative pronoun 'that one' used to pick out a person from a place (here: 'the one from...'); in coordinated pairs like this two singular 'quel' phrases function together as a plural subject. 'di' here means 'from' (origin). 'Portogallo' names the realm (Portugal). The second half repeats the same pattern with 'e' linking, 'di Norvegia' = 'from Norway'. 'lì' is the locative adverb 'there'. 'si conosceranno' is the 3rd-person plural future of the pronominal verb conoscersi: the si marks a reciprocal/passive-reflexive meaning ('they will recognize one another' or 'they will be known/recognized there').
segments.paradiso-19-g140: The initial e is 'and'. 'quel di Rascia' = 'the one/from Rascia' (Rascia = medieval Serbian region). 'che' introduces a relative clause 'who/which'. 'male' is an adverb here meaning 'badly' or 'illly' (i.e. suffering). 'ha visto' is the compound perfect (have + past participle) of vedere; idiomatically it can mean 'has experienced' rather than strictly 'has seen'. 'il conio' refers to coinage/minting (the coins or the act of minting). 'di Vinegia' = 'of Venice' (Vinegia = Venice). The whole relative clause thus says that the man from Rascia has had an ill experience with Venetian coinage (i.e. has been harmed/cheated by it).
segments.paradiso-19-g141: Oh is an exclamatory interjection. 'beata' is the adjective 'blessed' (here as a vocative description applied to the country as a person). 'Ungheria' is the country name 'Hungary' used as a vocative, personified.
segments.paradiso-19-g142: The clause begins with se ('if') introducing a wished-for conditional; non is the negation 'not'. si is the reflexive clitic marking the verb lasciare in its middle/passive sense ('allow oneself' or 'let oneself be ...'). lascia is 3rd-person singular present of lasciare ('to let/allow' here passive: 'permit oneself to be'); più means 'any longer' in negative contexts ('no longer'/'anymore'). malmenare is the infinitive 'to beat up; to mistreat' and completes the periphrasis 'non si lascia più malmenare' = 'does not allow herself to be mistreated any longer'.
segments.paradiso-19-g143: e is 'and'. beata is again the adjective 'blessed' applied to the country-personification. Navarra is the proper name 'Navarre'.
segments.paradiso-19-g144: se introduces a counterfactual/conditional clause ('if'). s’ is the reflexive clitic (si elided), here attached to armasse to form 'armarsi' ('to arm oneself'). armasse is the imperfect subjunctive (3rd singular) of armare used in the protasis of an unreal or wished-for conditional: 'if she were to arm herself' or 'if she would arm herself'. del is the contraction of di + il 'of the'/'from the'; monte is 'mountain'; che is the relative 'that/which'; la (here referring to Navarra) is the feminine article 'her' or 'it' depending on sense; fascia is a noun 'band/sash' or 'belt' used figuratively 'that girds/encircles her' — 'il monte che la fascia' = 'the mountain that encircles her'.
segments.paradiso-19-g145: E is 'and'. creder here is used impersonally ('one would think' / 'one would believe'); de' is the contracted preposition 'of/about' (de + il or de alone) marking the object of that impersonal thought. ciascun means 'each one' (each person/place). che introduces the content clause ('that'). già is an adverb 'already' emphasizing that the state is already in effect.
segments.paradiso-19-g146: per is the preposition 'for'/'because of' here introducing a reason. arra is given as a toponym in the gloss and functions as a place-name or technical term tied to the event ('for Arra' or 'on account of Arra'); di is the normal preposition 'of' linking to questo 'this' (questo = 'this matter/thing'), so per arra di questo roughly 'for Arra about this' / 'by reason of this Arra'.
segments.paradiso-19-g147: Niccosïa and Famagosta are the coordinated subjects (Nicosia and Famagusta). per la lor bestia = 'on account of / because of their beast' (per = 'because of' or 'for the sake of', la = definite article, lor = possessive 'their', bestia = 'beast/animal'). si is the reflexive clitic used with lamenti to form lamentarsi (they lament); lamenti is the verb form here meaning 'they lament/complain' (3rd-person plural). e joins a second verb garra (here 'garra' functions as a verb meaning 'cry out, make a noise' or similar) so 'they lament and cry out'.
segments.paradiso-19-g148: che is the relative pronoun 'which/that' referring back to 'bestia'. dal is the contraction da + il = 'from the' or 'at the'; fianco means 'side' or 'flank'. de l’ (de + l’) = 'of the'; altre here means 'the other' (archaic spelling l’altre = l'altra) referring to the other city; non is the negation 'not'; si scosta is the pronominal verb scostarsi in the 3rd-person singular present 'moves away/withdraws' — with the negation it means 'does not move away' or 'does not leave the side'. The clause asserts that the beast does not stray from the flank of the other.

sentences.paradiso-19-s29: What will the Persians be able to say to your kings, when they see that open volume in which all their contempts are written?
sentences.paradiso-19-s30: There you will see, among Albert's works, that one which will soon set the pen in motion, by which the kingdom of Prague will be left deserted.
sentences.paradiso-19-s31: There will be seen the grief which, over the Seine, is caused — by falsifying the coin — the one who will die suddenly from a blow to the hide.
sentences.paradiso-19-s32: There will be seen the pride that takes hold, which makes the Scot and the Englishman foolish, so that he cannot endure within his proper bounds.
sentences.paradiso-19-s18: Now who are you, who want to sit on a bench, to judge from a thousand miles away with the short sight of a handspan?
sentences.paradiso-19-s19: Certainly, to the one who squabbles with me, if Scripture were not against you, it would be a wonder to doubt.
sentences.paradiso-19-s22: The first will, which is good by itself, of itself (it being the highest good), never moved.
sentences.paradiso-19-s23: So just it is, insofar as that suits her: no created good draws her to itself; but she, radiating, produces him.
sentences.paradiso-19-s24: Just as the stork turns above the nest after she has fed her young, and as the one who has eaten looks back at her; so the blessed image was made, and so I lifted my eyelids — the blessed image moved its wings, urged by so many counsels.
sentences.paradiso-19-s25: Revolving, it sang and said: "As my notes are to you who do not understand them, so is the eternal judgment to you mortals."
sentences.paradiso-19-s26: Then those shining fires of the Holy Spirit quieted themselves again in the sign that made the Romans revered throughout the world; it resumed: "To this kingdom never ascended anyone who did not believe in Christ, neither before nor after he was nailed to the wood."
sentences.paradiso-19-s28: …who in the judgment will be much less near to him than one who does not know Christ; and such a Christian will condemn the Ethiopian, when the two groups separate, one rich in eternity and the other needy.
sentences.paradiso-19-s13: It is not light, unless it comes from the serene sky that is never disturbed; rather it is darkness or a shadow of the flesh or its poison.
sentences.paradiso-19-s14: That hiding-place which had concealed for you living justice is now opened to you enough; about which you used to raise the question so often; for you used to say: 'A man is born on the shore of the Indus, and there is no one there to speak of Christ, nor anyone to read or to write; and all his wishes and good deeds are, as far as human reason can see, without sin in life or in speech.'
sentences.paradiso-19-s15: He dies unbaptized and without faith:
sentences.paradiso-19-s16: Where is his fault, if he does not believe?
sentences.paradiso-19-s1: Before me appeared, with wings spread, the beautiful image that, in the sweet enjoyment, made the assembled souls glad; each seemed like a little spout in which a ray of the sun burned so fiercely that it was refracted in my eyes.
sentences.paradiso-19-s2: And that which I must describe here at once never carried a voice, nor was it written in our presence, nor was it ever grasped by imagination; for I saw, and even heard, the face speak, and in the voice 'I' and 'my' sounded, when in conception it was 'we' and 'our'.
sentences.paradiso-19-s3: And he began: 'Because I was righteous and pious, I am exalted here to that glory which does not allow itself to be overcome by desire; and on earth I left my memory so made that the people there, though wicked, commend it, but do not follow its history.'
sentences.paradiso-19-s4: Thus one single warmth from many embers is felt, just as from many loves only one sound came forth from that image.
sentences.paradiso-19-s5: Thereupon I added: “O perpetual flowers of eternal joy, whose every scent still seems but one to me, loosen for me, by breathing, the great fast that has long kept me in hunger, for I find no food for it on earth.”
sentences.paradiso-19-s6: I know well that, if in heaven another realm makes divine justice its mirror, yours does not receive it veiled.
sentences.paradiso-19-s7: You know how attentively I prepare myself to listen; you know what that doubt is which has for so long been a fast to me.
sentences.paradiso-19-s8: Like a falcon that springs from the cap, he turns his head and beats his wings, showing his desire and making himself beautiful; I saw that sign made, which was full of praises of divine grace, with songs such as those who rejoice up there know.
sentences.paradiso-19-s9: Then he began: "He who turned the sixth to the farthest edge of the world, and within it discerned so much that was hidden and manifest, could not so imprint his power on the whole universe that his Word would not remain in infinite excess."
sentences.paradiso-19-s10: And that certainly shows that the first proud one, who was the supreme of every creature, for not awaiting the light fell grievously; and from this it appears that every lesser nature is a short receptacle for that Good which has no end and measures itself with itself.
sentences.paradiso-19-s11: Therefore your vision, which it is fitting to be some ray of the Mind with which all things are filled, cannot by its nature be so powerful that its principle discerns far beyond what appears to it.
sentences.paradiso-19-s12: Therefore, in eternal justice the sight that receives your world, like an eye over the sea, turns inward within itself; for although from the shore it sees the bottom, in the open sea it does not see it; nevertheless it is there, but the depth hides its existence from it.
sentences.paradiso-19-s33: One will see the lust and the soft living of those of Spain and of those of Bohemia, who never knew nor wanted valor.
sentences.paradiso-19-s34: One will see at the Ciotto of Jerusalem his goodness marked with an i, when the opposite will mark it with an m.
sentences.paradiso-19-s35: One will see the avarice and the cowardice of those who watch the island of fire, where Anchises ended his long life; and, to give a sense of how little it is, his writing will be in truncated letters, which will record much in a small space.
sentences.paradiso-19-s36: And the filthy deeds of the barber and of the brother will appear to each one, who have made so illustrious a nation and two crowns into mere drafts.
sentences.paradiso-19-s37: And the one from Portugal and the one from Norway will be recognized there,
sentences.paradiso-19-s38: O blessed Hungary, if she would no longer allow herself to be mistreated!
sentences.paradiso-19-s39: and blessed Navarre, if she would arm herself with the mountain that girds her!
sentences.paradiso-19-s40: And one would think of each one that already, because of Arra concerning this, Nicosia and Famagusta, on account of their beast, lament and cry out, for it does not leave the side of the other.
